description_long: This object is a large, flat solar energy infrastructure installation featuring multiple rectangular solar panels arranged in a grid-like pattern. The panels are mounted on a robust, likely metallic frame with several black support structures distributed along the length. The surface of the panels is dark and reflective, typical of photovoltaic cells, while the frame and supports are matte black and gray. The installation includes a slightly curved section at one end, possibly for structural or aerodynamic purposes. The overall design suggests it is intended for stationary placement in an urban environment, such as on a rooftop or a dedicated ground area.
description: A large, grid-arranged solar panel installation with black supports and a slightly curved end, designed for stationary urban use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the grid of solar panels, prominent black supports, and the curved end section.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the installation, the tilt of the panels, and the distribution of black supports.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the panels, the support structures, and the curved end more clearly.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the panel tilt, support arrangement, and the overall flatness of the installation.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are tilted at approximately 20-25 degrees, orientation appears fixed, azimuth direction not clearly specified.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a robust metal frame and multiple black support posts, likely aluminum or steel.
attribute_array_configuration: Multiple rows of rectangular panels arranged in a grid, approximately 3 rows by 6 columns.
